sections.heading: The short answer
sections.body: A CIBIL score is a 3-digit number between 300 and 900 that tells lenders how reliable you are at repaying debt.

sections.heading: Why does it matter?
sections.body: When you apply for a loan or credit card, the bank checks your CIBIL score first. Score above 750 = easy approval and low interest rate. Below 600 = likely rejection.

sections.heading: What builds your score?
sections.body: 35% — paying on time. 30% — keeping credit card usage below 30% of limit. 15% — having old credit accounts. 10% — having different types of credit (card + loan). 10% — not applying for too many loans at once.

sections.heading: What damages your score?
sections.body: Missing EMI payments (even by 1 day). Using more than 30% of your credit card limit. Closing old credit card accounts. Applying for multiple loans in a short time.
